note: Submodule of xls_serialization. Decorates the xlsx encoder with an OpenSpout-based implementation for performance. No config/UI/permissions of its own. All cases are `recipe` (response-graded); no site mutation.

evals.prompt: My Drupal XLSX exports from xls_serialization run out of memory on large datasets. What submodule addresses this and how does it help?
evals.expected_output: Enable the xls_serialization_open_spout submodule; it replaces the xlsx encoder with an OpenSpout-based one that is faster and uses less memory than PhpSpreadsheet.

evals.prompt: How does this submodule take over Excel output — do I have to change my Views or REST configuration? Name the mechanism and the service it replaces.
evals.expected_output: Its service (OpenSpoutXlsxEncoder) decorates xls_serialization.encoder.xlsx and is tagged encoder/xlsx, so just enabling the module transparently swaps the backend; no config change needed. Disable it to revert.

evals.prompt: Installing this submodule fails a requirements check. Which external library must be present, and what version?
evals.expected_output: It needs the openspout/openspout library (^4); hook_requirements blocks install if the OpenSpout\Writer\XLSX\Writer class is missing.

evals.prompt: If I switch to the OpenSpout backend, which Excel features from the main xls_serialization encoder do I lose?
evals.expected_output: It drops features like custom document metadata, conditional formatting, header styling, and column/row auto-sizing — trading them for performance.

evals.prompt: Does the OpenSpout submodule replace both the xls and xlsx encoders, or only one? Which format string does it decorate?
evals.expected_output: Only the xlsx encoder (format `xlsx`) is decorated; the legacy `xls` encoder is unchanged.

evals.prompt: Can I enable xls_serialization_open_spout on its own? What module does it depend on?
evals.expected_output: No; it depends on the parent xls_serialization module (extends its Xlsx encoder) plus the openspout/openspout library.
